title: Words Per Conversation
description: The union rep wants to petition the Director about the twelve-word conversation limit. The petition is a document that communicates in words. Any document over twelve words per sentence is automatically flagged non-compliant. The system is the argument against itself, and it filters the argument.

offer.speaker: Union Rep
offer.text: You asked what I'd say with more words. Eleven, then:

offer.speaker: Union Rep
offer.text: I would say: help me write something they cannot ignore.

accept.speaker: Union Rep
accept.text: Twelve pages. Single-spaced. Every sentence too long to survive the filter.

accept.speaker: Union Rep
accept.text: Help me compress it. Without losing what it means. That's the job.

decline.speaker: Union Rep
decline.text: Understood. The draft keeps. Drafts are patient. Twelve words exactly, that.

in_progress.speaker: Union Rep
in_progress.text: Read it back shorter. Keep the dignity part. Cut me instead.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Union Rep
ready_to_turn_in.text: Form 99-Theta accepted the filing. Now we wait. Together, preferably.

complete.speaker: Union Rep
complete.text: Whatever happens to it now — it exists. Filed. On record. Unignorable.

complete.speaker: Union Rep
complete.text: You gave me more than words. Framework stays; fragments connect. Watch:

complete.speaker: Union Rep
complete.text: I think — six words — she can't stand gaps. Twelve more: she builds systems to fill all the gaps. Gaps scare her.

objectives.description: Hear the eleven words the union rep saved up.
Restructure the petition — twelve words per sentence, less than 10% of the argument lost.
Submit it through Form 99-Theta: Employee Expression Request.
Wait with him. That part matters too.

scriptweaver.description: Dialogue-only: the Scriptweaver refiles the petition in formal system notation — the Director's native language — bypassing the word-count filter into her personal queue. Her response is nine words: 'Noted. Valid point. Recalibrating communication protocols. Thank you.' And a tenth word that shouldn't be there, smaller: 'Sorry.'

unlocks_dialogue.note: Trust unlocked — he bypasses the 12-word limit via connected fragments. He knows three things: the foreman's watercolor archive location (feeds world4_the_watercolors), the Director's secondary monitoring hub address (optional dungeon room), and what the Director fears (gaps). His trust also grants informal counter-signature authority for world4_form_exception_alpha (labor clause predating the communication protocols).

cross_world_connection: The epilogue response, if the main quest completes first: twelve words exactly — 'Your concerns have been noted. Action pending further review. Thank you.' The system answers the petition about the system in the system's voice.
_wiring_notes: Step 2 emitter quest_world4_words_per_conversation_petition_restructured: the writing puzzle — a multi-exchange dialogue interact with the union rep (compress/expand choices). v1 can wire it as N sequential dialogue interacts (3 exchanges) before emitting; the Bard ('I know what compression costs') and Mage ('an optimization problem with a hard constraint') get flavor lines if in party — always true under strict-5, so both can fire. Step 3 emitter: Form 99-Theta filing interact at the complaints office (40 pages, 12 relevant, 28 identical — readable flavor). Post-quest slow-burn per doc: a brief union-rep check-in beat at the start of each subsequent main-quest chapter while the petition crawls through review — wire as optional dialogue variants keyed on chapter flags, not quest state. ALL dialogue for this NPC must respect the twelve-word frame: pre-trust lines are <=12 words per sentence BY DESIGN (verify in review if edited); post-trust keeps the frame but chains fragments.
